[[File:Rainbow stone.jpg|thumb]]Rainbow Stones are a type of currency in Finch. You can spend them in four shops to buy different things for your [[birb]]: 
*[[Clothing|Clothing]] ([[Mr. Prickles' Shop]])
*[[Furniture|Furniture and decorations]] for your [[birbhouse]] ([[Finkea Furnishings]])
*[[Colors]] to customize your birb's appearance ([[The Color Studio]])
*Travel to different [[Locations|locations]] for your Finch to explore ([[Travel with Sass]])

You can also spend Rainbow Stones on fixing your [[Streaks|streak]], or to refresh the daily shops, or pay the fee (200 stones) to send a gift to a [[Tree Towns|Tree Town]] friend.  

==Earning stones==
[[File:Rainbow stones - ways to earn.jpg|200x200px|right|alt=Ways to earn rainbow stones]]Rainbow Stones can be earned many ways, such as:
* completing [[Goals|goals]] 
* achieving full [[energy]]<ref>Increasing your [[Friendship|friendship level]] with your birb will earn more stones when an adventure begins.</ref>
* completing [[Quests|quests]] 
* opening chests from [[Seasonal Events|seasonal (monthly) events]] 
* selling items back to the store 
* sending [[Good Vibes|good vibes]]
* [[Invite|inviting]] real life friends to try Finch
* writing [[Reflections|reflections]] 
* ... and other [[Activities|activities]]

Players receive 65-80 free rainbow stones from [[Mr. Prickles]] once a day when they open the [[Mr. Prickles' Shop|outfit]] section of the [[shops]] tab. 

==Earning MORE stones== 
WARNING: Some people find that they become too focused on getting more stones and buying outfits, and stop paying attention to their Goals. This is a real problem. Keep your priorities in mind! 

Finch is not played against other players and is not funded by microtransactions, so it is morally up to you to decide how to use the game in support of your Self-care Goals. However, be kind to others - don't post pictures of all your rooms and outfits in the forums or social media. It hurts others, making them wonder why they don't have so much. Keep them for your private enjoyment. Also, please respect your friends - they may not want to hear these tips, in case it distracts *them* from their goals.

The following tips are NOT "cheating" per se. They take advantage of existing game mechanics. These tips are taken from the Finch subreddit ([https://www.reddit.com/r/finch/comments/1morbx5/feel_like_im_cheating/ example], [https://www.reddit.com/r/finch/comments/1kppc0z/i_just_discovered_something_i_didnt_know_about/ example]), which is moderated by Finch staff:<ref>The Finch subreddit rules say they will delete any posts about cheating. Since comments including these tips abound, they are obviously not cheating. The staff and volunteers on the Finch subreddit and Discord have enough time to 100% delete any mention of the wiki, which is a normal resource in any other game. So, clearly, they have enough time to censor anything that's "cheating."</ref>

*Mood - If you are having a bad day, choose the lowest value on the [[Mood logger|mood selector]] to get more stones for each click. 
*"Weight" your tasks - A counter on each goal goes up to 100. The counter is handy if you have to do something five times a day; you can set it to five. But if a specific task is especially hard for you, you can "weight" it by setting up more clicks for it (thus getting more stones.) For example, "vacuum the living room" might be worth 10 clicks, while "get the mail" might be 15. Or 5. It's up to you. The maximum is 100, and you can have multiple goals with 100.
*Goal of the Day<ref>[[File:GoaloftheDayCirclednew.jpg|thumb]]To activate Goal of the Day, click on a goal title and click the words "Goal of the Day" just above it. Click it off later when you have done all but 1-2. Go to a different goal and repeat.</ref> - A surprisingly increased number of stones are earned per click on your selected Goal of the Day. (The boosted value is random.) It's best to use this on goals with multiple clicks. The bonus expires when you "finish" a goal, whether it's a single click or 10 clicks or whatever. But you can maximize this by careful application to additional goals. Stop short of the final click; if it's 10 clicks, do only 9. Turn off Goal of the Day on that task and go to another multi-click goal. Turn it on as Goal of the Day. Again, stop short of doing all clicks. Repeat. 
[[File:RainbowStonesGoaloftheDay1-4.png|center|thumb]]{{Clear}}

The developers may decide to reduce the benefit from these tips. But until then, wiki users have as much right to them as Redditors and Discord users.
